content: Box Breathing (4-4-4-4): Inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 4, exhale for 4, and hold for 4. This technique is used by Navy SEALs to stay calm and focused under pressure.
4-7-8 Technique: Inhale for 4 seconds, hold for 7, and exhale forcefully for 8. This is highly effective for falling asleep and reducing acute anxiety.
Child's Pose (Balasana): A resting pose that gently stretches the hips, thighs, and ankles. It helps calm the mind and relieves stress and fatigue.
Cobra Pose (Bhujangasana): Strengthens the spine, stretches the chest and lungs, shoulders, and abdomen. It can help elevate mood and invigorate the heart.
Mindfulness Meditation: Focus on your breath as it moves in and out of your body. When your mind wanders, gently bring your attention back to the breath without judgment.
Loving-Kindness Meditation (Metta): Focus on developing feelings of goodwill, kindness, and warmth toward others and yourself. Repeat phrases like 'May I be happy, may I be healthy'.
The Importance of Consistency: Even 5 minutes of daily practice is more effective than an hour once a week. Consistency builds the neural pathways for calm.
Hydration and Mind: Dehydration can lead to increased cortisol levels (the stress hormone). Drinking water regularly helps maintain emotional balance.
Tree Pose (Vrksasana): Improves balance and stability in the legs. On a mental level, it helps cultivate focus and concentration.
Alternate Nostril Breathing (Nadi Shodhana): Helps balance the left and right hemispheres of the brain, calming the nervous system and preparing the mind for meditation.
